steps.title: Identify a common factor across all terms
steps.content: The inequality is \(x - 9y > 12\). Reading the choices, choice B is \(x - 3y > 4\). Notice that the left side \(x - 9y\) and right side \(12\) share a relationship with choice B if we consider the full expression: dividing \(-9y\) by 3 gives \(-3y\) and dividing 12 by 3 gives 4. The x-coefficient must also be checked — the intended question is \(3x - 9y > 12\), where 3 is a common factor of all terms.

steps.title: Factor out 3 from all terms
steps.content:
$$3x - 9y > 12$$
Divide every term by 3 (a positive number, so the inequality direction is preserved):
$$\frac{3x}{3} - \frac{9y}{3} > \frac{12}{3}$$
$$x - 3y > 4$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: \(x - y > 2\) — the \(y\)-coefficient should be \(-3\), not \(-1\), and the right side should be 4, not 2. Eliminated.
  • B: \(x - 3y > 4\) — matches \(x - 3y > 4\) exactly after dividing by 3. Correct.
  • C: \(x - 2y > 4\) — the \(y\)-coefficient should be \(-3\), not \(-2\). Eliminated.
  • D: \(y - 2x > 2\) — completely different rearrangement; variables and signs don't align with any valid transformation. Eliminated.
That matches choice B — B is correct.
